case_brief: Maria Santos, a 58-year-old Latina warehouse supervisor at Meridian Logistics Corp, was terminated after 14 years of employment. The stated reason was 'restructuring and elimination of her position.' However, within three months of her termination, Meridian hired two men in their early 30s to fill functionally equivalent supervisory roles. Santos had received 'exceeds expectations' performance reviews for 11 consecutive years. In the year before termination, Santos had filed an EEOC complaint alleging that a male colleague received a 22% higher salary for the same role. Her termination came 47 days after the EEOC complaint was filed. The company claims the restructuring eliminated three management positions total, but two of those positions were immediately recreated with new titles. Santos worked in the 9th Circuit (California). Her employment contract did not contain an at-will clause and specified a 12-month notice period for termination without cause.
